He had a good job, a very dependable man, and the city honored him enough that after 20 years of faithful service they gave him a certificate of award for his faithful service. He'd go out and donate work after work hours, see, that's why they did it I guess.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: He wasn't like some of them, you lay down on strike, a lot of them, you know. No, he just worked the way he figured it out, he'd keep good order out there on the maintenance crew.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: And when I went down there, I was flat broke, I had this place, but that's all I had, I didn't have no money. And around here you couldn't get no money, I mean wages. I worked in, sold sacks around all over the country. Yeah, yeah, yeah, that's what I was doing.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: I went and lived with Simon and his wife until I got a defense job. That's what I did.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: I stayed there, I think it was three or four weeks before I got a job, and I got tired of laying around, I said, Simon, you know the city, could you wrestle someplace that I could get to work, before I could get on to something else?

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: So he got me on what they call an iron farm, in them days you see there was a lot of war stuff, and they had to make these castings. Great big wheel castings. Iron firemen, that was the name. I got the book, I had to join the union. You showed me that book.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: Yeah.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: So you got you an iron fireman. Iron fireman, and worked, and so when I got on that, then they come up a place for sale. I told Simon and his wife, my sister -in -law Grace, she's going to come up this summer I guess, she said she would, I got a card for her Christmas. Anyway, I said, I'd like to get some work in the heat. He said, well, he said, the only place I know right now, he says, down there at the iron farm, and she said, pretty hard work, but I said, I don't care, just till I get work.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: And I got on, only 81 cents an hour there, and it worked like a beaver, but I handled it in a little foreman, he said, you know, Elmer, he said, what you're doing?

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: I said, no, well, he said, you're doing just two men's work.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: And so that gave me a leeway then, finally, I had to hoist it up, you know how a truck bed is high, just about all in, and I said, hey, you got a bar here that I can get five or six of them castings on at one clip?

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: See, we had a steam hoist there, that way I said I could maybe make him holler, or sweat.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: So he finally did, and I said, what are you going to do with it?

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: I said, I'm going to take several at a time, I said, so I can get him, cover him up, and make him quit hollering for more.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: Boy, I had that fella sweating, and, well, then one day, the wife had heard over the radio that they wanted, oh, I took up a school before I went there, I said, yeah, I figured I had to learn something about iron by taking up this sittling welding.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Where at?

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: Here in Moscow, right down on, well, now let's see, I guess right off of Main Street, about where Rosauers is, that's where it was at that time. And I happened to know the fella that we'd boarded, when we went to school here in Moscow, we'd boarded with the family there, his mother and his dad, Elmer Humphrey, and he was running this school.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: And when I went down there to get on, you know, God, Elmer, he said, you're getting too old, take that. See, in 40, that was in 42, I was 54 then. Well, I said, I'd like to get something, I said, I've got to get some kind of a trade so I can get better wages around Moscow here. I said, it's only in harvest time when I sold sacks I get any kind of wage. So he said, alright, I'll try your art, and boy I passed on it. I made it go of it.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: My wife is here.

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: Oh is she?

speaker: Helmer Ringsage
words: Well, you'll have to come back.
